articles.title: Secret agent, who was about to blow the lid off UFO secrets, died 5 years ago. A whistleblower has now raised suspicion
articles.summary: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released.

en.title: Secret agent, who was about to blow the lid off UFO secrets, died 5 years ago. A whistleblower has now raised suspicion
en.summary: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released.

de.title: Der Geheimagent, der UFO-Geheimnisse enthüllen wollte, starb vor fünf Jahren. Ein Whistleblower hat nun Verdacht geäußert
de.summary: Kevin Childress, ein 30-jähriger Kriminalermittler des Energieministeriums, starb 2021 unerwartet in seinem Haus in Georgia, wobei die Behörden Komplikationen durch COVID-19 anführten. UFO-Whistleblower Luis Elizondo behauptet, Childress sei vor seinem Tod gesund gewesen und habe gewarnt, dass das DOE eine wichtige Rolle in UAP-Programmen gespielt habe. Elizondo sagt, das Energieministerium versuche ihn zum Schweigen zu bringen und stellt fest, dass weder eine öffentliche Autopsie noch eine detaillierte Todesursache veröffentlicht wurden.

articles.title: Trump declassifies 162 UFO files for public to judge the 'evidence'
articles.summary: S. government released 162 declassified UFO files from 1948 to 2026, including PDFs, videos, and images from the Department of Defense, FBI, NASA, and State Department, to increase transparency about unidentified flying objects. The archive contains reports from astronauts and pilots but no definitive evidence of extraterrestrial life, and includes FBI documents about a suspected Nazi aircraft described by Paul Peyerl, though the agency states it contains no conclusions.

en.title: Trump declassifies 162 UFO files for public to judge the 'evidence'
en.summary: S. government released 162 declassified UFO files from 1948 to 2026, including PDFs, videos, and images from the Department of Defense, FBI, NASA, and State Department, to increase transparency about unidentified flying objects. The archive contains reports from astronauts and pilots but no definitive evidence of extraterrestrial life, and includes FBI documents about a suspected Nazi aircraft described by Paul Peyerl, though the agency states it contains no conclusions.

de.title: Trump gibt 162 UFO-Akten für die Öffentlichkeit frei, um die „Beweise“ zu beurteilen
de.summary: Die US-Regierung hat zwischen 1948 und 2026 162 freigegebene UFO-Dateien veröffentlicht, darunter PDFs, Videos und Bilder des Verteidigungsministeriums, des FBI, der NASA und des Außenministeriums, um die Transparenz über nicht identifizierte Flugobjekte zu erhöhen. Das Archiv enthält Berichte von Astronauten und Piloten, aber keine eindeutigen Beweise für außerirdisches Leben, und enthält FBI-Dokumente über ein mutmaßliches Nazi-Flugzeug, das von Paul Peyerl beschrieben wurde, obwohl die Agentur angibt, dass sie keine Schlussfolgerungen enthalten.

summaries.film-how-to-watch-sleeping-dog-online-st: Journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell discusses his new documentary with NBC News correspondent Gadi Schwartz. The documentary is titled “Sleeping Dog.” It explores Corbell’s navigation of government secrecy. It also covers whistleblowers and his search for truth about UFOs.
summaries.alien-whistleblower-claims-us-has-dismem: Investigative writer Timothy Alberino claimed that the U.S. government has recovered alien bodies and hides them in underground military bases, according to an interview on the platform Redacted. He said that crash‑retrieval operations involving non‑human craft and bodies are conducted outside the oversight of elected officials and are controlled by private aerospace contractors and highly compartmentalised defence programmes. Alberino referenced anecdotal eyewitness accounts of “gray aliens” at alleged retrieval sites, describing dismembered bodies and transparent fluids, and cited former intelligence official David Grusch’s 2023 testimony that the U.S. had recovered “non‑human biologics.” No publicly available evidence has been produced to substantiate Alberino’s allegations, and U.S. defence agencies have not confirmed the existence of such programmes. Congressional interest in UAP investigations remains high, with lawmakers calling for greater transparency, but the Pentagon maintains that its All‑domain Anomaly Resolution Office has not produced verified evidence of extraterrestrial activity.
summaries.secret-agent-who-was-about-to-blow-the-l: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released. The FBI is investigating a series of missing and dead scientists linked to secret nuclear and UFO programs, including the disappearance of retired General William Neil McCasland. Other scientists, such as David Wilcock, have also died under suspicious circumstances, adding to the pattern of unexplained losses.
summaries.program-former-pentagon-linked-physicist: Hal Puthoff, a former CIA‑funded physicist, claimed on a podcast that the United States may have recovered four distinct alien species from crashed UFOs, though he has not seen the evidence himself. His remarks echo earlier testimony by former Air Force intelligence officer David Grusch, who said the U.S. possessed “non‑human biologics” from unidentified anomalous phenomena crash sites. Puthoff’s associate Eric Davis described the recovered beings in intelligence reports as “gray,” “Nordic,” “insect‑like,” and “reptilian,” all humanoid with two arms and two legs, but no detailed evidence was provided. The documentary “The Age of Disclosure,” directed by Dan Farah, features interviews with military officials, intelligence figures, and political leaders discussing alleged UAP encounters, government secrecy, and a supposed secret “Legacy Program.” Despite these claims, no verified public evidence has emerged, and U.S. officials continue to state that there is no confirmed evidence linking UAP sightings to extraterrestrial origins.
summaries.trump-declassifies-162-ufo-files-for-pub: The U.S. government released 162 declassified UFO files from 1948 to 2026, including PDFs, videos, and images from the Department of Defense, FBI, NASA, and State Department, to increase transparency about unidentified flying objects. The archive contains reports from astronauts and pilots but no definitive evidence of extraterrestrial life, and includes FBI documents about a suspected Nazi aircraft described by Paul Peyerl, though the agency states it contains no conclusions. A retro‑styled website hosts the documents, highlighting sightings such as a bright object over Kazakhstan and a light seen by Buzz Aldrin, but experts note the material is largely unremarkable and partially redacted. President Trump said the release lets the public judge the evidence, while some members of Congress seek further disclosure. Additional files include an FBI interview of a drone pilot who saw a linear object in 2023 and a NASA photo from Apollo 17 of three dots, with the Pentagon noting no consensus but a preliminary analysis suggesting a physical object.

articles.title: Trump declassifies 162 UFO files for public to judge the 'evidence'
